from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of airport‑related triples into a readable, single‑sentence description."""
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Mapping predicates to clause fragments (subject omitted)
    clause_map = {
        # Airport specific
        "cityServed": "serves the city of {object}",
        "location": "is located in {object}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "lies {object} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "lies {object} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "lies {object} metres above sea level",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {object}",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {object} metres",
        "runwayName": "has runway {object}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "runway surface is {object}",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "has runway number {object}",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway {object} metres long",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway {object} feet long",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "second runway surface is {object}",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "third runway surface is {object}",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "fourth runway surface is {object}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "fifth runway surface is {object}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": "has a third runway {object} feet long",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "has a fourth runway {object} feet long",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "has fifth runway number {object}",
        # General geographic / political
        "country": "is in {object}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {object}",
        "capital": "has capital {object}",
        "largestCity": "has largest city {object}",
        "countySeat": "has county seat {object}",
        "leader": "has leader {object}",
        "leaderParty": "belongs to party {object}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {object}",
        "mayor": "has mayor {object}",
        "chief": "has chief {object}",
        "owner": "is owned by {object}",
        "headquarter": "has headquarters at {object}",
        "regionServed": "serves region {object}",
        "jurisdiction": "lies in jurisdiction {object}",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language {object}",
        "language": "has language {object}",
        "demonym": "has demonym {object}",
        "currency": "uses currency {object}",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {object}",
        "areaCode": "has area code {object}",
        "foundingYear": "was founded in {object}",
        "ceremonialCounty": "is in ceremonial county {object}",
        "administrativeArrondissement": "is in administrative arrondissement {object}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "has IATA code {object}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "has ICAO code {object}",
        "locationIdentifier": "has location identifier {object}",
        "nativeName": "has native name {object}",
        "hubAirport": "has hub airport {object}",
        "aircraftHelicopter": "operates helicopter {object}",
        "aircraftFighter": "operates fighter {object}",
        "attackAircraft": "operates attack aircraft {object}",
        "transportAircraft": "operates transport aircraft {object}",
        "battle": "participated in battle {object}",
        # Fallback for any other predicate
        # (will be handled later)
    }
    # Build a single‑sentence description preserving the order of triples.
    parts: list[str] = []
    prev_subject: str | None = None
    for t in triples:
        # Clean object: remove surrounding quotes and format numbers nicely
        obj = t.object
        if isinstance(obj, str):
            obj = obj.strip()
            if obj.startswith('"') and obj.endswith('"'):
                obj = obj[1:-1]
            try:
                num = float(obj)
                if num.is_integer():
                    obj = str(int(num))
                else:
                    obj = str(num)
            except ValueError:
                pass
        tmpl = clause_map.get(t.predicate)
        clause = tmpl.format(object=obj) if tmpl else f"{t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')} {obj}"
        if prev_subject is None or t.subject != prev_subject:
            # start a new clause with the subject
            parts.append(f"{t.subject} {clause}")
        else:
            # same subject – just add the predicate phrase
            parts.append(clause)
        prev_subject = t.subject

    if not parts:
        return ""
    if len(parts) == 1:
        return f"{parts[0]}."
    # join with commas, using Oxford comma before the final part
    return f"{', '.join(parts[:-1])}, and {parts[-1]}."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
